title: JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS - 211info
text: COMMUNITY WORKS JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an access point for being placed on Jackson County's Centralized Interest List for housing. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless, with a focus on serving people experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, or trafficking, as well as youth. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Helpline (541) 779-4357 Español (541) 779-2393 ext 209 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for screening appointment and ask to be put into coordinated entry. Households will be placed on the Centralized Interest List for available housing. Most appointments will be in-person rather than by phone. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Jackson County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Location is in the RE/MAX building, in a complex on the corner of E Barnett Road and Black Oak Drive. Use the RE/MAX entrance and take stairs or elevator to the lower floor. Services: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Domestic Violence Issues Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Human Trafficking Issues Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Sexual Assault Issues Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Youth Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - TRANSITIONAL LIVING PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY INFORMATION SERVICES - LABOR AND SEX TRAFFICKING SURVIVOR ADVOCACY INFORMATION SERVICES - SEXUAL ASSAULT SURVIVOR ADVOCACY MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - HELPLINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY WORKS
